meta.description: Design system governance rules for @wyllo/ui. Encodes design intent — not just what tokens exist, but how they should relate to each other.

foregroundHierarchy.description: Text colors have an emphasis sequence. foreground is full emphasis (any content). muted-foreground is de-emphasized (descriptions, helper text, h3/h4 as subheadings). muted-foreground must never appear on h1 or h2.

tokens.foreground: Full emphasis — headings, body text, labels, data. No restrictions.
tokens.muted-foreground: De-emphasized — descriptions, helper text, placeholders, h3/h4 subheadings.

violations.fix: Use foreground (text-foreground) for h1 and h2

violations.reason: primary-foreground is designed for contrast against primary backgrounds
violations.fix: Use foreground or muted-foreground for standard surfaces

surfaceHierarchy.description: Backgrounds have a stacking order: background (canvas) → card (elevated) → secondary (nested) → muted (subtle) → accent (hover) → popover (floating).

violations.reason: accent is reserved for hover/active states
violations.fix: Use bg-secondary or bg-muted for static differentiation

borderHierarchy.description: Borders have tiers: border (structural), border-subtle (low-emphasis), input (interactive surfaces), ring (focus).

tokens.border-subtle: Low-emphasis separators inside surfaces. Must be less prominent than border.
tokens.input: Interactive surfaces — form controls, dialogs, any UI where the user makes a decision

violations.pattern: ring token used outside focus/focus-visible state
violations.reason: ring is exclusively for focus indicators
violations.fix: Use border or input for non-focus borders

violations.reason: All borders must use semantic tokens
violations.fix: Use border, border-subtle, or input tokens

elevationCoherence.description: Shadows map to component scale. Small components use light shadows. Large overlays use heavy shadows.

violations.pattern: elevation-overlay or elevation-popover on a small component (badge, button, input)
violations.reason: Heavy shadows on small elements break visual scale
All shadows must use semantic elevation tokens
violations.fix: Use elevation-surface, elevation-floating, elevation-overlay, or elevation-popover

violations.pattern: Raw shadow primitive (shadow-y1, shadow-y2, shadow-y6, shadow-y16)
violations.reason: Primitives are for token definitions in globals.css only

semanticColorPairing.description: Status colors are 3-token contracts: base (background tint), -border, -foreground. They must be used as complete sets and never mixed across schemes.

tokenRoles.base: Background tint — light in light mode, dark in dark mode. NOT for text or icons.
tokenRoles.-foreground: Text and icon color. This is the visible content color.
tokenRoles.-solid-foreground: Text on solid background (destructive, brand only).

violations.pattern: text-destructive, text-success, text-warning, text-brand, text-review (base token used for text/icons)
violations.reason: The base token is a background tint (e.g., rose-50 in light mode — nearly invisible as text). Use the -foreground variant for content.
violations.fix: Replace text-destructive with text-destructive-foreground, text-success with text-success-foreground, etc.

violations.pattern: Mixing schemes — e.g., bg-success with text-warning-foreground
violations.reason: Schemes are designed as cohesive sets. Cross-scheme pairing breaks visual coherence.
violations.fix: Use tokens from the same scheme (bg-success + border-success-border + text-success-foreground)

violations.pattern: Mixing solid and tinted variants on the same element
violations.reason: Solid variants are inverted (bold bg + light text). Tinted variants are subtle (light bg + bold text). Mixing them produces unreadable contrast.
violations.fix: Use either the tinted set (destructive/destructive-border/destructive-foreground) or the solid set (destructive-solid/destructive-solid-foreground), not both.

typographyConventions.description: The type system uses weight classes (420/520/620/660) and scale utilities. Arbitrary weights and sizes break the system.

weights.420: Body text (p, p-lg, p-sm, form-label, form-control)

violations.reason: The design system uses numeric weights (420/520/620/660), not named weights
violations.fix: Use font-[420], font-[520], font-[620], or font-[660], or the corresponding utility class (p, label-md, h2, data-md)

violations.reason: The type scale is defined by utility classes, not arbitrary values
violations.fix: Use the scale: text-xs, text-sm, text-base, text-lg, text-xl, text-2xl, text-3xl

primitiveLeakage.description: Raw primitive tokens (gray-*, violet-*, orange-*) and hardcoded color values should never appear in component source files. Primitives are for token definitions in globals.css only.

violations.pattern: Raw primitive token in component file (gray-*, violet-*, orange-*)
violations.reason: Components must use semantic tokens. If no semantic token exists, flag it as a gap.
violations.fix: Replace with the appropriate semantic token (foreground, muted-foreground, background, etc.)

violations.pattern: Hardcoded color value (#hex, rgb(), rgba(), oklch(), hsl())
violations.reason: All colors must come from the token system
violations.fix: Use a semantic color token or add a new semantic token if one doesn't exist

violations.pattern: Tailwind palette class (text-blue-500, bg-red-400, border-gray-200, etc.)
violations.reason: Standard Tailwind palette classes bypass the design system
violations.fix: Use semantic tokens from the design system
